text.title: Engagement Pattern Memory Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Engagement Pattern Memory application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: There MUST be exactly one memory file per active engagement; shared files rejected.
rule.rationale: Shared files leak conventions between clients.

rule.statement: Memory file MUST contain sections {repo_conventions, reviewer_preferences, deploy_quirks, recurring_traps, glossary, resolved_questions}; ad-hoc structure rejected.
rule.rationale: Un-structured memory cannot be loaded selectively by LLM agents.

rule.statement: Memory MUST be updated within 30 min of session end; later writeback loses fidelity.
rule.rationale: Memory written hours later is reconstructive.

rule.statement: Session-start MUST load the active engagement's memory file; absence rejected.
rule.rationale: Sessions without memory load repeat last week's mistakes.

rule.statement: Memory file MUST NOT contain client PII beyond the engagement name; rejected on leak.
rule.rationale: Memory files are agent-readable and may be backed up off-host.
